element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn individual team member strengths/weaknesses before committing to events
Resource management—players must decide spending based on medal accumulation vs. team needs

element_introduction_order.element: Individual Events (Hammer Throw, Roof Top Jumping)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Single-player scoring mechanics; teams alternate turns; ranking by points
Single-elimination tournament format; direct opponent competition with heat progression
Direct combat elimination; culmination of physical competition; face Team Thornley members
Wealthy rival team; represents peak difficulty spike; completion unlocks ending sequence

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension: safe shopping phases alternate with escalating competitive events; tension peaks during head-to-head elimination matches and climaxes at Team Thornley final

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Shopping mall accessible before events; power-up purchases are discretionary based on team composition and anticipated event difficulty

difficulty_curve.early_game: Individual events with turn-based scoring; lower pressure; allows team assessment and early resource accumulation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of single-elimination head-to-head events (Hurdles, Swimming); moderate competitive pressure; tournament bracket narrows field
difficulty_curve.late_game: Fighting Scene finals; direct combat mechanics; peak difficulty represented by Team Thornley members; requires accumulated resources and character synergy
notable_spikes: Transition from individual to head-to-head events
First encounter with Team Thornley in finals bracket
Fighting Scene event (different mechanic from sports contests)

notable_level_patterns.description: Five distinct event types presented in fixed order: 400m Hurdles, Hammer Throw, Swimming, Roof Top Jumping, Fighting Scene. Each event type recurs across competition rounds with increasing difficulty via opponent quality and resource constraints.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: 400m Hurdles appears in early heats, repeated in finals with Team Thornley

notable_level_patterns.description: Head-to-head events use single-elimination format. Players progress through qualifying heats against other teams, then face Team Thornley representative for first place. Advancement unlocks subsequent events.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Swimming event requires defeating two opponents before championship match vs Team Thornley

notable_level_patterns.description: Medal awards from each event fund shopping phase. Strategic spending on power-ups impacts subsequent event performance. Spending too early limits options for later difficult matchups.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early Hammer Throw results determine medal budget for power-ups before Swimming event
